notes.preset: desktop sets a 1350x940 viewport, no network throttling, and disables mobile emulation — use for table rendering benchmarks where layout matters
notes.chromeFlags: --incognito disables all Chrome extensions (analytics, ad-blockers, devtools panels) which would otherwise inflate scripting time and skew results
notes.cpuSlowdownMultiplier: 4x CPU throttling simulates a mid-tier device (see https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse/blob/main/docs/throttling.md); desktop preset defaults to 1x — 4x is closer to real user hardware for call-center laptops
notes.onlyCategories: performance skips accessibility, best-practices, and SEO audits — cuts run time ~60% and avoids false failures from benchmark pages that intentionally skip those concerns
notes.iterations: Run 5+ times and take the median to reduce variance from GC pauses and OS scheduling
notes.networkProfile: Desktop preset uses no throttling; for mobile simulation add --throttling-method=simulate
notes.lighthouseVersion: Use lighthouse@13 to match Chrome DevTools built-in version — v12 and v13 use different audit keys (dom-size vs dom-size-insight); analyze_lighthouse.py handles both
notes.devtoolsExport: For pages that crash CLI with PROTOCOL_TIMEOUT (basic-table with 4x throttling), export Lighthouse JSON from Chrome DevTools panel and feed to analyze_lighthouse.py directly
